description_long: This house features a traditional, elongated L-shaped layout with a prominent hip roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The exterior walls are a light beige or cream color, accented by dark trim around the windows and base. Multiple chimneys rise from the roof, indicating several fireplaces or stoves inside. The windows are symmetrically arranged, mostly rectangular, and appear to be double-hung with dark frames. A small dormer with a gabled roof projects from the central section, adding architectural interest. The structure suggests a single-story residential building, likely constructed from masonry or brick with a plaster finish.
description: A single-story, L-shaped house with a hip tile roof, beige walls, and symmetrical windows, featuring multiple chimneys and a central dormer.
description_view_0: This view highlights the L-shaped layout, the hip roof with reddish tiles, and three visible chimneys, as well as the dormer on the central section.
description_view_1: From this angle, the dormer and the intersection of the two wings are prominent, with more windows visible on the shorter wing.
description_view_2: This side shows the long facade with evenly spaced windows and two chimneys, emphasizing the building's length.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the extended length of the house, the hip roof, and a series of windows along the longer wall.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with a simple door, small overhang, and minimal decorative elements
